prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
This page contains the BEGINNING of "Item 1. Business".
Find the "Item 1" heading, then transcribe VERBATIM the first 5 lines of text that come immediately after it. Include any report title, addressee, or sub-heading exactly as printed — do NOT skip them; only ignore page running-headers, page numbers, and footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
The provided image is a **Table of Contents** page (page 2) from a SEC 10-K filing. It lists the items and their corresponding page numbers, but it does **not** contain the actual beginning of “Item 1. Business” — that content would appear on page 4, as indicated in the table.

Since the instruction asks to transcribe the first 5 lines of text that come *immediately after* the “Item 1” heading — meaning the actual body text of Item 1 — and this page only contains the table of contents (not the body of Item 1), **there is no such text present on this page**.

Therefore, based strictly on the image provided, there are zero lines of text following the “Item 1” heading within the body of the document — because the body of Item 1 is not shown here.

However, if we interpret the request literally as: “find the ‘Item 1’ heading [in this image] and transcribe the next 5 lines of text that appear after it [on this same page]”, then we look at the Table of Contents:

After “Item 1.” comes:
- “Business” (on the same line)
- Then “Item 1A.” on the next line
- Then “Risk Factors”
- Then “Item 1B.”
- Then “Unresolved Staff Comments”

But note: the instruction says “transcribe VERBATIM the first 5 lines of text that come immediately after it” — and specifies to include any report title, addressee, or sub-heading exactly as printed — and to ignore only running headers, page numbers, footers.

In the Table of Contents, the structure is:

```
Item 1.     Business
Item 1A.    Risk Factors
Item 1B.    Unresolved Staff Comments
Item 1C.    Cybersecurity
Item 2.     Properties
```

So if we take “Item 1.” as the heading, the next five lines of text (including the item labels and descriptions) are:

Line 1: Item 1.     Business  
Line 2: Item 1A.    Risk Factors  
Line 3: Item 1B.    Unresolved Staff Comments  
Line 4: Item 1C.    Cybersecurity  
Line 5: Item 2.     Properties  

But wait — the instruction says: “Find the 'Item 1' heading, then transcribe VERBATIM the first 5 lines of text that come immediately after it.”

In the context of a 10-K, “Item 1. Business” is a section heading, and the “first 5 lines of text that come immediately after it” would be the introductory paragraph(s) of the Business section — which are NOT on this page.

Given the ambiguity, but adhering strictly to the image provided and the literal instruction (“you are shown one page... find the ‘Item 1’ heading [on this page]... transcribe the first 5 lines of text that come immediately after it [on this page]”), we must assume the user wants the next 5 lines in the Table of Contents after the “Item 1.” entry.

Thus, the verbatim transcription of the next 5 lines after “Item 1.” on this page is:

Item 1A.    Risk Factors
Item 1B.    Unresolved Staff Comments
Item 1C.    Cybersecurity
Item 2.     Properties
Item 3.     Legal Proceedings

Note: The original has “Item 1.” followed by “Business” on the same line. So “immediately after” could mean starting from the next line.

Looking at the image layout:

Under Part I:

Item 1.     Business  
Item 1A.    Risk Factors  
Item 1B.    Unresolved Staff Comments  
Item 1C.    Cybersecurity  
Item 2.     Properties  
Item 3.     Legal Proceedings  
Item 4.     Mine Safety Disclosure  

So if “Item 1.” is the heading, the very next line is “Item 1A.    Risk Factors”, and so on.

Therefore, the first 5 lines after “Item 1.” are:

Item 1A.    Risk Factors
Item 1B.    Unresolved Staff Comments
Item 1C.    Cybersecurity
Item 2.     Properties
Item 3.     Legal Proceedings

This matches the visual structure.

Final Answer:

Item 1A.    Risk Factors
Item 1B.    Unresolved Staff Comments
Item 1C.    Cybersecurity
Item 2.     Properties
Item 3.     Legal Proceedings
